title: About this fixture
intro: This page is here to make Tentman feel concrete: a real singleton with markdown, structured highlights, and one reusable image block.
body:
## Why this page exists

The test app is meant to show a small but believable content model. It should be plain enough that structural issues are obvious and varied enough that new Tentman features have somewhere honest to land.

- The homepage works like an index.
- The blog is a directory-backed collection.
- The FAQ page demonstrates nested collections.
- This page uses a shared image block.

## Content components

The official name for the newer markdown feature is **content components**. In this fixture, markdown can link readers toward :doc-link[the FAQ page]{href="/faq" variant="strong"} or :doc-link[the blog index]{href="/blog" variant="plain"} using semantic source markers instead of stored final HTML.

highlights.label: Singleton pages
highlights.detail: About, FAQ, and Contact each use file-backed content with different field shapes.

highlights.label: Collections
highlights.detail: Posts live as individual markdown files and the frontend mirrors Tentman ordering.

highlights.label: Nested content
highlights.detail: The FAQ schema includes repeatable sections with repeatable question items inside each one.

gallery.alt: Pinned planning notes and sketches on a studio wall
gallery.caption: A reusable image block gives the fixture one clear place to exercise assets and captions.

gallery.alt: A bright desk with a keyboard, coffee, and design comps
gallery.caption: The site stays deliberately restrained so content-model changes show up immediately.

gallery.alt: A workshop board with cards arranged in lanes
gallery.caption: This block is shared rather than page-specific, which makes it useful as a regression surface.
